stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced Apr 22, 2026: net profit Rs 1,353.8 cr (+16% YoY) but missed Bloomberg consensus of Rs 1,509 cr (~10% miss); revenue Rs 15,076 cr (+12.6% YoY). FY26 EBIT Rs 7,152 cr (+39.2% YoY). EBIT margin 13.8% (+70 bps QoQ, +330 bps YoY) - tenth consecutive quarter of margin expansion. Final dividend Rs 36/share, total FY26 dividend Rs 51 (highest ever). FY26 TCV $3.79B (+42% YoY) - highest in 5 years. Headcount fell ~2,000 in Q4. Stock down 1.79% on Apr 27, trading near Rs 1,358.
stocks.sector_context: Indian IT sector under pressure in 2026 - 'AI deflation' concerns weighing on traditional services deal sizes. TCS outperformed peers on growth and deal momentum. Infosys -22% YTD with muted FY27 guidance. Sector waiting for demand inflection; client spending hesitation persistent. Q4 results season exposed sector vulnerability to AI-driven disruption.
stocks.peer_comparison: QoQ constant currency growth: TCS 1.2% (lead), TECHM 0.6%, Wipro 0.2%. TECHM mid-pack but lagging market leader TCS. Margin expansion of 330 bps YoY among the strongest in peer group. Profit miss makes it relative laggard vs TCS (which beat). Headcount cut sharper than peers - more aggressive cost lever.
red_flags: Q4 FY26 net profit MISS - Rs 1,354 cr vs Rs 1,509 cr Bloomberg consensus (~10% miss)
Headcount fell ~2,000 in Q4 - signals demand caution
AI-driven deal compression risk for traditional IT services
Sector beta negative - Nifty IT a 2026 underperformer
Stock weak post-results despite margin expansion
catalysts: Highest-ever FY26 dividend Rs 51/share - record date pending
Record FY26 TCV of $3.79B underwrites FY27 growth resumption
Margin trajectory toward management's 15% EBIT target
Sector turnaround if FY27 guidance from peers surprises positively

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 reported Apr 24-25, 2026: consolidated PAT Rs 16,971 cr (-12.55% YoY) impacted by West Asia war hit to O2C and oil & gas EBITDA. Q4 gross revenue Rs 325,290 cr (+12.9% YoY); FY26 gross revenue all-time high Rs 11,75,919 cr (+9.8% YoY); FY26 EBITDA +13.4% YoY. Final dividend Rs 6/share. Jio: digital subscribers +16% YoY, 5G subs +40% growth. Retail Q4 revenue Rs 98,000 cr (+14% adjusted for RCPL demerger) - highest ever. Stock fell ~1.2% on Apr 27 to intraday low Rs 1,312.
stocks.sector_context: Diversified conglomerate with segments diverging. O2C/oil & gas hit by Middle East geopolitical disruption. Digital and retail segments strong. Indian large-cap diversifieds remain a relative safe haven amid IT/financial volatility. Crude price volatility a near-term swing factor for O2C.
stocks.peer_comparison: Q4 PAT decline of -12.55% lags peers like TECHM (+16%), Bajaj Finance (+22.8%). FY26 revenue at all-time high but earnings momentum weak. Jio competes with Bharti Airtel (outperforming on tariffs); retail with DMart/Trent. O2C exposure makes it a relative drag vs pure-play upstream/refining peers in current crude environment.
red_flags: Q4 FY26 PAT decline of 12.55% YoY
O2C and oil & gas EBITDA pressured by West Asia conflict
Stock under selling pressure post-results
Net profit decline despite revenue growth signals margin compression
Petchem segment vulnerable to sustained high crude
catalysts: FY26 final dividend Rs 6/share - record date pending
Potential Jio IPO updates
FY27 outlook and capex commentary at AGM
Refining margin expansion from oil dislocation could lift O2C in Q1 FY27
Telecom tariff hike potential

stocks.recent_news: Hit 52-week high of Rs 293.20 on Apr 28, 2026 (+3%); trading around Rs 301.60. New CFO appointed effective May 1, 2026. Board approved 2nd interim dividend of 125% (Rs 6.25/share, total payout Rs 7,863 cr). Approved Rs 25 cr JVC and up to Rs 264.93 cr IGGL support. APPCB issued stop-production order on Mori#05 well (Apr 25) after blowout and alleged environmental violations. Earlier fire incident at SHP Platform, Mumbai High on Apr 3 (extinguished, 10 minor injuries). Net crude realisation $61.63/bbl in last reported quarter (-15% YoY).
stocks.sector_context: Upstream oil rallying strongly. JM Financial sees Brent ~$85/bbl near-term on refinery disruptions in Middle East/Russia (~6 mb/d cut in global runs in April 2026). Geopolitical risk premium $5-10/bbl. Every $1/bbl rise in crude boosts ONGC earnings 1-2%. Currently market price discounting only ~$65/bbl realisation - implies upside if crude holds. Risk: sustained high crude historically triggers windfall tax/SAED on Indian upstream.
stocks.peer_comparison: Oil India also rallied (+4% on Apr 28 to Rs 492.30). Both pure-play upstream beneficiaries of high crude. Analyst targets: ONGC Rs 340 (JM Financial Buy), Oil India Rs 585. ONGC is larger; Oil India typically higher beta to crude. Versus OMCs (BPCL/HPCL/IOC) - ONGC structurally insulated from refining-margin compression. Versus RIL O2C - cleaner upstream exposure.
red_flags: Mori#05 stop-production order from APPCB (Apr 25) - environmental/regulatory penalty risk
SHP Platform fire on Apr 3 - second operational HSE incident in a month
Crude oil realization down 15% YoY in last reported quarter
Sustained Brent above $100 historically triggers windfall-tax/SAED reimposition
Stock near 52-week high - limited cushion if tax overhang materializes
CFO transition May 1 adds short-term execution uncertainty
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results upcoming - first read on Brent benefit window
New CFO transition effective May 1, 2026
Interim dividend Rs 6.25/share - total payout Rs 7,863 cr
JM Financial TP Rs 340 implies ~13% upside
Sustained Brent above $80/bbl
JVC and IGGL capital deployment

stocks.recent_news: Trading around Rs 316.25 on Apr 29, 2026. Stock down ~15% in FY26 despite executing two major strategic milestones (ITC Hotels demerger effective Jan 1, 2025; FMCG scaling). Q4 FY26 results scheduled for May 5, 2026 board meeting. Q3 FY26 showed steady profit growth, modest revenue uptick, strong cigarette margins, mixed FMCG/agri/hotels. 52-week range Rs 390-528 (significantly below highs).
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG outperforming - +11.73% in April vs Nifty50's +7.4%. Defensive rotation amid IT sell-off. FMCG basket rallying with HUL, Nestle, Dabur, Tata Consumer all up 1-4% intraday Apr 29. Sector seen as resilient long-term wealth creator with rate-cut tailwind and rural demand recovery.
stocks.peer_comparison: ITC has materially underperformed FMCG peers in FY26 (-15% vs sector index +11.73% in April alone). HUL and ITC together account for >45% of Nifty FMCG weight, but ITC has been the laggard. Post-demerger SOTP target by Motilal Oswal/Jefferies Rs 520-570 (cigarettes 25x, FMCG 60x, agri 15x, paperboards 12x) - implies significant relative undervaluation if execution delivers.
red_flags: Stock down ~15% YTD in FY26 vs FMCG sector index +11.73% in April alone
Cigarette excise duty hike risk in Budget 2027
Trading well below 52-week high of Rs 528
Volume migration to illicit cigarette market on each price hike
Sector underperformer despite Nifty FMCG rallying
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results on May 5, 2026 (consensus revenue Rs 18,500-19,200 cr, PAT Rs 5,000-5,400 cr)
Final dividend announcement with Q4
Post-demerger SOTP re-rating potential to Rs 520-570
FMCG sector momentum continuing
Strong cigarette margins continuing per Q3 trend

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: net profit Rs 5,581 cr (+22.8% YoY); NII +21.2% YoY. AUM grew 22% YoY to ~Rs 4.16 lakh cr (vs Rs 3.42 lakh cr prior year). Customer franchise at 119.33 million (vs 101.82 mn YoY) - +17.5 mn additions. Board approved fundraise via debt and recommended dividend. Stock around Rs 8,100, vs 52-week high Rs 9,230 / low Rs 6,650. Down ~4.6% YTD despite recent rally.
stocks.sector_context: NBFC sector at recovery inflection point in 2026. RBI rate-cut cycle beginning - direct beneficiary. MFI stress peaking. Vehicle finance demand recovering. RBI proposed Rs 1 lakh cr asset mark for upper-layer NBFC classification (Apr 10, 2026) - regulatory tightening at the top tier. NBFCs offering FD rates 6.7-7.3% vs banks 6.45-6.50%.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest NBFC by AUM and most valued; trades at premium ~6.5-7x book value. Cholamandalam Rs 1.99 lakh cr AUM - roughly half of BAJFINANCE. HDB Financial (HDFC Bank-backed) emerging competitor in underserved markets. Bajaj Finance leads sector on scale, profitability, digital adoption. Q4 PAT growth +22.8% comfortably ahead of most large NBFC peers.
red_flags: Premium valuation of 6.5-7x book - high bar for sustained outperformance
Stock still down ~4.6% YTD despite Q4 beat
Sector-wide MFI/unsecured stress, though peaking
Upper-layer NBFC regulatory tightening risk under RBI's proposed framework
Trading below 52-week high of Rs 9,230
catalysts: Q4 FY26 beat - PAT +22.8%, AUM +22%, customer franchise +17.5 mn
Board-recommended dividend - record date pending
Debt fundraise approved
RBI rate-cut cycle direct beneficiary
NBFC sector recovery - MFI stress peaking, vehicle finance reviving
